articles.title: 'Country's politics utterly rotten': Cong on Maha MP defections

articles.title: BJP has 'anti-Dalit' mindset, alleges Punjab minister Cheema
articles.description:
Chandigarh, Jun 23 (PTI): Punjab Finance Minister Harpal Singh Cheema on Tuesday alleged that the BJP's "anti-Dalit" mindset has been exposed during its president Nitin Nabin's recent state visit.

Referring to a recent BJP event, Cheema claimed that senior Dalit leaders such as former Union ministers Vijay Sampla and Som Parkash were made to stand among the audience while others were seated on stage, alleging clear discrimination.

Nabin's three-day visit to Punjab concluded on Monday.

Cheema alleged that the BJP respects only an "elite" class while Dalits, workers and the poor are "systematically sidelined and humiliated".

The senior AAP leader emphasised that the Scheduled Caste community, forming nearly 34 per cent of Punjab's population, has witnessed this approach firsthand, and claimed that the BJP is "anti-Punjab, anti-farmer" and its "anti-Dalit face" has now been fully exposed, adding that the people of Punjab will respond firmly to such politics.

Cheema said, "According to National Crime Records Bureau 2024 data, nearly 65 per cent of crimes against Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the country were reported from four BJP-ruled states including Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Bihar and Rajasthan. This reflects the BJP government's failure to protect Dalits and demonstrates the party's deep-rooted prejudice against marginalized communities." "During a recent party event in Punjab, senior SC leaders such as former Union minister Vijay Sampla and former Union minister Som Parkash were made to stand among the audience while top leadership was seated on stage. This incident has exposed the BJP's real face before the people of Punjab.

"Leaders who have served the party for decades and represented the Scheduled Caste community were denied even basic respect," he claimed.

"The episode clearly demonstrates the BJP's discriminatory mindset towards Dalits, workers and economically weaker sections. The BJP only respects a privileged and elite class. Those who represent labourers, Dalits and the poor are sidelined and humiliated," he alleged. PTI CHS KVK KVK

(This story is published as part of the auto-generated syndicate wire feed. No editing has been done in the headline or the body by ABP Live.)

articles.title: 'Are you a DEMOCRATIC SOCIALIST?': Kennedy's fiery clash with witness over inflation, rising prices

articles.title: HP govt invokes ESMA on HRTC ahead of drivers, conductors strike
articles.description: The Himachal Pradesh government has declared HRTC services essential under ESMA to counter a proposed strike by drivers and conductors from June 25. The govt is hiring temporary drivers and has appealed to unions to withdraw their strike call.

articles.title: MoS George Kurian's resignation fuels Cabinet rejig buzz
articles.description: Union Minister of State for Minority Affairs George Kurian has resigned, sparking speculation of a Cabinet reshuffle. His Rajya Sabha term concluded without renomination, and President Murmu accepted his resignation. Prime Minister Modi's meeting with the President further fueled these discussions. Potential new roles for Kurian, including a governorship, are being considered as other ministers transition to state party leadership or await decisions on their future.

articles.title: U.S. eases restriction on Iran’s World Cup team, allowing travel two days before next match
articles.description: The U.S. relaxes travel restrictions for Iran's World Cup team, allowing them to arrive two days before their next match.

articles.title: Best Proverb of the Day: 'You are permitted in time of great danger to walk with the devil until...'-A life lesson hidden in an ancient proverb that changes how we see difficult choices
articles.description: Life often presents difficult choices where ideal solutions are absent. A timeless proverb suggests that in times of great danger, temporary alliances, even with undesirable entities, are permissible to ensure survival. This wisdom emphasizes practicality over rigid ideals, urging individuals to recognize when a compromise is necessary for safety. Crucially, it warns against lingering too long once the danger has passed, stressing the importance of returning to one's values.

articles.title: South Carolina, New York, Maryland, Utah primary elections results: Polls closed in 4 states, who is winning and who is losing
articles.description: South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson won a runoff election on Tuesday, swiftly routing the candidate initially endorsed by President Donald Trump to be the Republican nominee for governor.

articles.title: Idiom of the Day: 'A dime a dozen'- Meaning, origin, and inspiring life lessons on the harsh truth about commonality and value in today's world
articles.description: In today's world, many things are easily found and lack uniqueness, a concept captured by the idiom 'a dime a dozen.' This phrase, originating from 19th-century America, highlights that abundance often diminishes value. The article emphasizes that true worth lies not in commonality but in qualities like character, dedication, and authenticity, urging individuals to cultivate what makes them exceptional rather than blending in.

articles.title: After BrahMos, India may have found its next military bestseller
articles.description: India’s defence export story, long centred on the BrahMos supersonic missile, may be expanding to include a new system gaining global attention. Reports suggest the United Arab Emirates (UAE) has shown interest in India’s BrahMos missile as well as the Akashteer air defence system, signalling potential growth in India’s defence export portfolio.

articles.title: 'Curb extremism, ensure minorities' safety': MEA to B'desh on 'Hindu deities disrespected' report
articles.description: India on Tuesday expressed hope that the government in Bangladesh would act against extremist elements and ensure the safety of the minority community following reports of desecration involving Hindu deities and their images that have triggered protests across Bangladesh. Addressing a weekly media briefing, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said, “We have seen reports from Bangladesh regarding acts of desecration involving Hindu deities and their images, which have sparked protests. We expect the Bangladeshi government to curb the extremists there and guarantee the safety of the minority community.”

articles.title: India's scathing reply to Pakistan's military threats, rights abuses
articles.description: India hit back at Pakistani Defence Minister Khawaja Asif's military threats, calling them a desperate bid to hide domestic failings and rights violations. MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal highlighted Pakistan's suppression of protests in PoK.

articles.title: Shinde faction planning its own 'Shiv Sena Bhavan' near Matoshree? Here's what we know
TO BE DELETED
articles.description: Russia has released new footage of its Tu-160 strategic bombers conducting patrol flights over the Barents and Norwegian Seas, underscoring Moscow's long-range strike capabilities amid ongoing tensions with NATO. Known as the "Blackjack" by NATO, the Tu-160 is Russia's largest and fastest strategic bomber. The nuclear-capable aircraft can reportedly fly up to 14,000 kilometers and reach speeds exceeding 2,200 km/h, making it a key component of Russia's strategic deterrence force. The patrols took place over international waters near NATO's northern flank, an area closely watched by Western militaries. While such flights are a routine part of Russia's military operations and do not necessarily indicate preparations for an attack, they serve as a powerful demonstration of Moscow's ability to project force far beyond its borders.

articles.title: 'TRACKING TOP COMMANDERS': Israel Claims Hezbollah Secret Ops In Lebanon
articles.description: Is Iran-backed Hezbollah planning a major strike on Israeli military commanders? The Israel Defence Force (IDF) issued a warning of a growing threat from Hezbollah. According to reports, the IDF believes the Iran-backed group has rebuilt an intelligence and surveillance network in southern Lebanon and is using advanced technology to track senior Israeli commanders. Military investigations into recent attacks on high-ranking officers reportedly found indications that Hezbollah may be using drones, observers, cameras, and sophisticated radio-tracking capabilities to identify command activity, particularly during nighttime operations. Israeli officials also claim that Iran continues to provide Hezbollah with advanced drones and intelligence-gathering equipment.
